channel.title: MikeCast — Daily Briefing
channel.description: Your daily AI-powered news briefing. Personalized news across AI & Tech, Business & Markets, Companies, and NY Sports.
channel.itunes:author: MikeCast

item.title: MikeCast — March 06, 2026
item.description:
The financial landscape remains volatile as weak jobs data compounds the economic uncertainty fueled by Middle East tensions, impacting both stock markets and oil prices. Meanwhile, tech giants like SoftBank and Google make headlines with major financial maneuvers and controversial AI developments. In sports, New York teams are making strategic moves with the Yankees eyeing high-profile signings and the Giants actively pursuing new talent.
AI & TECH • Stocks Drop as Weak Jobs Report Adds to Uncertain Outlook • SoftBank eyes up to $40 billion loan to fund OpenAI investment, Bloomberg News reports • Google Search rolls out Gemini’s Canvas in AI Mode to all US users
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Top Fed Official, Wary of Inflation, Calls for Extended Rate Pause • Enterprises Are Spending 15% More on Software in 2026, Thanks to AI. Here's How to Profit. • Global oil benchmark Brent crude breaks above $90 a barrel amid Iran war, U.S. crude tops $89
COMPANIES • BofA Revalues Tesla On Future Robotaxi Profits. Cybercabs Spotted At Factory. • Magnificent 7 Stock Report: Apple Leads, NVIDIA Fades, Microsoft Crashes • Apple introduces the new MacBook Air with M5
NY SPORTS • MLB trade rumors: New York Yankees could sign $330 million Philadelphia Phillies franchise superstar to f • Minnesota Twins at New York Yankees Game Story, Scores/Highlights • Yankees news: Oswaldo Cabrera set to return to field Friday
item.itunes:title: MikeCast — March 06, 2026
item.itunes:subtitle: The financial landscape remains volatile as weak jobs data compounds the economic uncertainty fueled by Middle East tensions, impacting both stock markets and oil prices. Meanwhile, tech giants like SoftBank and Google make headlines with major financi...
item.itunes:summary:
The financial landscape remains volatile as weak jobs data compounds the economic uncertainty fueled by Middle East tensions, impacting both stock markets and oil prices. Meanwhile, tech giants like SoftBank and Google make headlines with major financial maneuvers and controversial AI developments. In sports, New York teams are making strategic moves with the Yankees eyeing high-profile signings and the Giants actively pursuing new talent.
AI & TECH • Stocks Drop as Weak Jobs Report Adds to Uncertain Outlook • SoftBank eyes up to $40 billion loan to fund OpenAI investment, Bloomberg News reports • Google Search rolls out Gemini’s Canvas in AI Mode to all US users
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Top Fed Official, Wary of Inflation, Calls for Extended Rate Pause • Enterprises Are Spending 15% More on Software in 2026, Thanks to AI. Here's How to Profit. • Global oil benchmark Brent crude breaks above $90 a barrel amid Iran war, U.S. crude tops $89
COMPANIES • BofA Revalues Tesla On Future Robotaxi Profits. Cybercabs Spotted At Factory. • Magnificent 7 Stock Report: Apple Leads, NVIDIA Fades, Microsoft Crashes • Apple introduces the new MacBook Air with M5
NY SPORTS • MLB trade rumors: New York Yankees could sign $330 million Philadelphia Phillies franchise superstar to f • Minnesota Twins at New York Yankees Game Story, Scores/Highlights • Yankees news: Oswaldo Cabrera set to return to field Friday

item.title: MikeCast — March 05, 2026
item.description:
OpenAI's rapid advancements with GPT-5.4 highlight the ongoing race in AI capabilities, while controversy brews over military applications. Market volatility persists amid geopolitical tensions, with oil prices surging due to the Iran conflict. Major firms like Google and Amazon face significant operational and legal challenges, underlining the dynamic landscape of tech and business.
AI & TECH • OpenAI introduces GPT-5.4 with more knowledge-work capability • OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with native computer use mode, financial plugins for Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets • Meet the A.I. Prospectors Tapping a Billion-Dollar Gusher
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Oil Jumps, Stocks Drop as Investors Grapple With Iran War’s Impact • Mortgages in 47 seconds: Better’s new ChatGPT app targets lenders Rocket and UWM • Dow futures tick higher, but index heads for worst week since October on oil surge: Live updates
COMPANIES • Lawsuit Filed Against Google and Alphabet Over Alleged AI Chatbot Safety Failures • Microsoft: Buy The Dip, Or Regret It Forever (NASDAQ:MSFT) • Is Google Stock Still Cheap or Is It Time to Let Go?
NY SPORTS • MLB trade rumors: New York Yankees could sign $330 million Philadelphia Phillies franchise superstar to f • Minnesota Twins at New York Yankees Game Story, Scores/Highlights • New York Yankees’ Hot Trade Rumor Just Doesn’t Add Up

NY SPORTS • MLB trade rumors: New York Yankees could sign $330 million Philadelphia Phillies franchise superstar to f • Minnesota Twins at New York Yankees Game Story, Scores/Highlights • New York Yankees’ Hot Trade Rumor Just Doesn’t Add Up

item.title: MikeCast — March 04, 2026
item.description:
Today's headlines are dominated by strategic shifts and settlements in the tech industry, with Google and Epic Games resolving their long-standing app store dispute, leading to a significant reduction in commission rates. In business, AI infrastructure spending is surging, reflecting the sector's growing importance. Meanwhile, Amazon and Apple unveil innovations that could reshape their market positions. In sports, the Yankees gear up for the season with promising performances and strategic considerations.
AI & TECH • Google and Epic announce settlement to end app store antitrust case • Trump Announces A.I. Industry Pledge to Pay for Power • Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies,’ report says
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Broadcom beats on earnings and guidance as AI revenue doubles • Live updates: War powers vote fails in the Senate, allowing Trump to continue Iran strikes • Enterprises now write $100M checks for AI infrastructure, says VAST Data VP
COMPANIES • Amazon’s new AI experience helps sellers visualize and grow their business in real time—here's how it works • Apple launches new MacBook Pro with M5 Pro and M5 Max chips • Say hello to MacBook Neo
NY SPORTS • Giancarlo Stanton solid in Yankees spring training debut: ‘Nice, prototype first day’ • New York Yankees at Boston Red Sox Game Story, Scores/Highlights • Should the Yankees sign Jazz Chisholm Jr. to a long-term extension?

In business, AI infrastructure spending is surging, reflecting the sector's growing importance. Meanwhile, Amazon and Apple unveil innovations that could reshape their market positions. In sports, the Yankees gear up for the season with promising performances and strategic considerations.

item.title: MikeCast — March 03, 2026
item.description:
Today's headlines highlight the intensifying conflict in the Middle East, its economic repercussions, and significant technological advancements. Automakers face the challenge of adapting to electric and autonomous vehicle trends, while the global economy braces for shocks from geopolitical tensions. Meanwhile, Apple and Amazon navigate new product launches and operational disruptions, respectively.
AI & TECH • Automakers’ Dilemma: Invest in New Tech or Stick to Gas Cars? • An Emboldened Israel Is Seizing Opportunities to Remake Region • Global Markets Tumble as Iran War Intensifies
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Tariffs Confound Small Businesses Again • Global Economy Is Facing the Prospect of Another Profound Shock • 5 Takeaways on America’s Boom in Billionaires
COMPANIES • Apple introduces the new iPad Air, powered by M4 • Everything Apple announced today: iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Air • Amazon says drones damaged three facilities in UAE and Bahrain
NY SPORTS • MLB Gameday: Yankees 5, Phillies 3 Final Score (03/01/2026) • Yankees invite veteran OF Randal Grichuk to spring training • How to Watch Knicks vs. Raptors, Injury Report, Preview

item.title: MikeCast — March 02, 2026
item.description:
Today's news is dominated by escalating tensions in the Middle East as the U.S. commits more troops to Iran, influencing both global politics and markets. In tech, the Pentagon strengthens its ties with innovators, while Apple pushes forward with new product releases. Meanwhile, the rising cost of energy and shifts in U.S. political strategies offer a glimpse into the evolving landscape of business and governance.
AI & TECH • The Pentagon’s Favorite Tech Guy Is This Hawaiian Shirt-Wearing Founder • OpenAI Amends A.I. Deal With the Pentagon • Reporters Seek Comment. What Happens Next May Surprise You.
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Trump Says He Will End Boycott of White House Correspondents’ Dinner • What Rising Oil Prices Could Mean for Drivers in the U.S. • They’re Hiring at U.S.A.I.D. Just Not Anyone Who Worked There.
COMPANIES • Apple introduces iPhone 17e • Apple unveils new iPad Air with M4 chip, RAM upgrade, plus C1X and N1 • Super PAC Owned by Facebook Parent Company Meta Spends Big in Texas Primaries
NY SPORTS • Here are the Yankees' 2026 Top 30 prospects • Yankees invite veteran OF Randal Grichuk to spring training • MLB trade rumors: New York Yankees look at $4.2 million Washington Nationals star as shortstop option whi

item.title: MikeCast — March 01, 2026
item.description:
Today's landscape is marked by geopolitical upheavals and technological shifts. The assassination of key Iranian leaders, following a collaborative intelligence effort between the U.S. and Israel, is shaking global politics and regional stability. Meanwhile, the tech world is witnessing intriguing shifts with a nostalgic return to the iPod and strategic moves at the Pentagon involving OpenAI. In business, remote work innovations and the impact of geopolitical events on oil markets are in focus.
AI & TECH • Bring On Defunct: The iPod Enthralls Young Music Listeners • At the Pentagon, OpenAI is In and Anthropic Is Out • The C.I.A. Helped Pinpoint a Gathering of Iranian Leaders. Then Israel Struck.
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Are ‘Bossware’ Tools Tracking You? • What It’s Like to Run a Fully Remote Company With a Four-Day Workweek • 40 Years and 10,000 Broadcasts In, They’re Still Live on Air
COMPANIES • iPhone and iPad approved to handle classified NATO information • Apple News promotes left-leaning media outlets -- as it shuts out conservative sites entirely: study • Meta Takes Legal Action Against Scam Advertisers
NY SPORTS • Cashman: Young arms, eventual return of Cole have Yanks 'capable of great things' • Blackburn throws four shutout innings in Yankees’ win • Game Preview: San Antonio Spurs vs New York Knicks

item.title: MikeCast — February 28, 2026
item.description:
Today's news highlights significant tensions in technology and geopolitics. The tech industry's clash with President Trump over AI usage underscores a pivotal moment for AI's role in national security. Simultaneously, U.S.-Iran tensions escalate dramatically with coordinated airstrikes by the U.S. and Israel, marking a critical juncture in international relations.
AI & TECH • Crypto’s Richest Man Details His Secret Talks, Prison Time and Humbling Comedown • Silicon Valley Rallies Behind Anthropic in A.I. Clash With Trump • Pentagon Standoff Is a Decisive Moment for How A.I. Will Be Used in War
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Netflix Lost Warner. Maybe That’s a Good Thing. • Abrupt Change for Warner Bros. Prompts Many Grim Faces • QAnon Faithful See Validation in the Epstein Files
COMPANIES • What is behind Apple recent drop in value today • Meta Takes Legal Action Against Scam Advertisers • How Meta Executives Talked About Child Safety Behind the Scenes
NY SPORTS • The most interesting players on the new ABS challenge leaderboard • Yankees see ‘real opportunity’ for Randal Grichuk with roster battle taking shape • Yankees bring fireworks to Fort Myers in drubbing of the Twins

item.title: MikeCast — February 27, 2026
item.description:
Today's news highlights significant geopolitical and technological shifts. Donald Trump's foreign policy strategies raise global tensions, particularly with Iran, while Elon Musk's vision of a "sustainable abundance" challenges economic norms. Meanwhile, AI's impact on traditional industries, from India's tech sector to the global 3-D printing market, continues to reshape business landscapes.
AI & TECH • They Helped Women Fight Online Abuse. They Were Barred From the U.S. • A World Where All Is Free? That’s Elon Musk’s Theory of ‘Sustainable Abundance.’ • Elon Musk’s Secret Web of Companies in Texas
BUSINESS & MARKETS • For CNN, a Change in Ownership Means a Suddenly Uncertain Future • Tech, TV, Movies and News: Ellisons on Brink of Colossal Empire • The Rise and Fall of a 3-D Printing Empire
COMPANIES • Meta Takes Legal Action Against Scam Advertisers • Meta executive warned Facebook Messenger encryption plan was 'so irresponsible', shows court filing • Update on our organization
NY SPORTS • Stanton determined to play through pain, even though he 'can't open a bottle' • Giancarlo Stanton raises eyebrows with ‘can’t open bag of chips’ comment • Yankees news: Giancarlo Stanton can’t open a bottle due to pain, wants full season

item.title: MikeCast — February 26, 2026
item.description:
In the tech world, Nvidia's remarkable financial performance and the pervasive influence of artificial intelligence are major highlights. The geopolitical landscape is tense with U.S.-Iran relations on edge, while Trump's policy maneuvers continue to stir domestic and economic discussions. In business, companies like Aston Martin are grappling with tariff impacts, and Meta's strategic partnerships underline the evolving AI infrastructure landscape.
AI & TECH • Nvidia’s Quarterly Profit Hits $43 Billion on Strong A.I. Chip Sales • A.I. Complicates Old Internet Privacy Risks • When Chatbots Are Used to Plan Violence, Is There a Duty to Warn?
BUSINESS & MARKETS • ‘Z’? ‘Zo’? ‘Mam’? A New Mayor Needs a Nickname. • Kalshi Says ‘MrBeast’ Employee Violated Insider Trading Rules • For America’s 250th, Should the Coin of Trump’s Realm Be Gold?
COMPANIES • Today’s obituary: Mary Craven, 79, guided North Syracuse’s ‘Apple Muffin Gang’ in 1987 • Why Apple Stock Ascended Today • Meta and AMD Partner for Longterm AI Infrastructure Agreement
NY SPORTS • Nationals vs. Yankees (Feb 25, 2026) Live Score • Yankees to honor CC with jersey retirement, plaque ceremony on Sept. 26 • One Ring Would Make Jalen Brunson a Knicks God

item.title: MikeCast — February 25, 2026
item.description:
Today’s news highlights the evolving landscape of AI and tech, with Netflix diversifying into podcasts and Wayve securing a major investment for autonomous vehicles. In business, debates swirl around AI's economic impact, while geopolitical tensions continue to shape energy markets. In the corporate world, Apple's new App Store requirements and Meta's major partnership with AMD are in focus. Meanwhile, New York sports teams are navigating roster challenges and player performances.
AI & TECH • Podcasts? TV Talk Shows? Netflix Just Hopes They’re Hits. • These Tools Say They Can Spot A.I. Fakes. Do They Really Work? • Wayve, an A.I. Driverless Car Start-Up in Europe, Raises $1.2 Billion
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Bleak Research Report Stokes A.I. Debate on Wall St. • In the Lap of Luxury: 5 New Hotels Aim to Indulge • Abu Dhabi’s State Oil Company Looks Beyond Oil
COMPANIES • Apple expands age assurance tools as new App Store requirements roll out in several regions • Meta and AMD Partner for Longterm AI Infrastructure Agreement • Meta executive warned Facebook Messenger encryption plan was 'so irresponsible', shows court filing
NY SPORTS • Knicks Roster Is Full, But Moves Might Not Be Done • ‘Passive’ Karl-Anthony Towns Under Fire After Knicks Loss to Cavaliers • Giants NFL free agency 2026: Will LB Micah McFadden have a place in New York?

item.title: MikeCast — February 24, 2026
item.description:
In today's briefing, the tech industry faces scrutiny as revelations about Jeffrey Epstein's ties to Microsoft surface, while OpenAI expands its enterprise reach through new partnerships. Meanwhile, the business landscape is shifting with regulatory pressures on unemployment benefits and significant investments in healthcare innovation. On the companies front, Apple's manufacturing plans mark a strategic shift, and Facebook faces internal criticism over its encryption policies. In sports, New York teams are making headlines, with impressive performances from the Yankees and updates on the Knicks and Devils.
AI & TECH • How Jeffrey Epstein Ingratiated Himself With Top Microsoft Executives • Is It Safe to Travel to Mexico Right Now? • OpenAI lands multiyear deals with consulting giants in enterprise push
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Trump Administration Tells States It Aims to ‘Reimagine’ Jobless Benefits • Centauri Therapeutics Extends Series A to £30M With £6M Investment From AMR Action Fund to Support Progression of Lead Clinical Candidate
COMPANIES • Apple accelerates U.S. manufacturing, with Mac mini production coming later this year • Apple’s Steve Jobs Would Have Turned 71 Today • Meta executive warned Facebook Messenger encryption plan was 'so irresponsible', shows court filing
NY SPORTS • Yanks' 6-foot-7 sluggers Judge (TWICE), Jones open spring with HRs • New York Knicks vs Milwaukee Bucks Feb 27, 2026 Game Summary • New Jersey Devils Prospect Update: First to 20 Edition

item.title: MikeCast — February 23, 2026
item.description:
Today’s news highlights the ongoing challenges and innovations in technology and business. Chinese AI firms are reportedly using data from Anthropic's Claude to enhance their own AI models, reflecting global competition in AI development. Meanwhile, U.S. financial markets experience volatility with a significant drop in the Dow, and Tesla faces scrutiny over data transparency. In sports, Olympic gold medalist Jack Hughes' return generates excitement among New Jersey Devils fans.
AI & TECH • Chinese AI companies 'distilled' Claude to improve own models, Anthropic says
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Dow Loses 821 Points to Open Nvidia Week: Stock Market Today • Top Fed Official Sees Little Effect on Rate Outlook From Supreme Court’s Tariff Ruling
COMPANIES • Tesla is having a hard time turning over its FSD traffic violation data
NY SPORTS • Here’s how much it costs to see Olympic gold medalist Jack Hughes return to the Devils Wednesday
item.itunes:title: MikeCast — February 23, 2026
item.itunes:subtitle: Today’s news highlights the ongoing challenges and innovations in technology and business. Chinese AI firms are reportedly using data from Anthropic's Claude to enhance their own AI models, reflecting global competition in AI development. Meanwhile, U....
item.itunes:summary:
Today’s news highlights the ongoing challenges and innovations in technology and business. Chinese AI firms are reportedly using data from Anthropic's Claude to enhance their own AI models, reflecting global competition in AI development. Meanwhile, U.S. financial markets experience volatility with a significant drop in the Dow, and Tesla faces scrutiny over data transparency. In sports, Olympic gold medalist Jack Hughes' return generates excitement among New Jersey Devils fans.
AI & TECH • Chinese AI companies 'distilled' Claude to improve own models, Anthropic says
BUSINESS & MARKETS • Dow Loses 821 Points to Open Nvidia Week: Stock Market Today • Top Fed Official Sees Little Effect on Rate Outlook From Supreme Court’s Tariff Ruling
COMPANIES • Tesla is having a hard time turning over its FSD traffic violation data
NY SPORTS • Here’s how much it costs to see Olympic gold medalist Jack Hughes return to the Devils Wednesday
